fix: prevent zombie claude processes and duplicate error fix sessions (#63)

- Fix race condition in error fix triggering: set fix_in_progress flag
  atomically in _trigger_error_fix before spawning the worker, preventing
  multiple concurrent Claude CLI processes from being launched by the
  periodic _check_errors timer and post-fix retrigger.

- Prevent zombie processes: add finally blocks to both _error_fix_worker
  and _claude_worker that call proc.kill()/proc.wait() to ensure child
  processes are always reaped, even when exceptions occur during stdout
  streaming.

- Switch stderr from PIPE to DEVNULL in both Popen calls to prevent
  deadlocks where the stderr pipe buffer fills up while we're blocking
  on stdout iteration, which could cause the child to hang indefinitely.

- Use RLock instead of Lock for ErrorManager to prevent deadlocks when
  update_fix_stage (which acquires the lock) is called from within a
  block already holding the lock.

- Guard all fix_in_progress checks (in _check_errors, autofix on
  command, post-fix retrigger) with proper lock acquisition to prevent
  TOCTOU races.

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

fix: add marketplace.json and fix plugin.json for remote loading (#13)

Three issues prevented the plugin from loading on cursor.com/agents:

1. Missing .cursor-plugin/marketplace.json — required for team marketplace
   discovery. Without it, Cursor shows 'No plugins found in this marketplace'.

2. Invalid author.url in plugin.json — the plugin schema (additionalProperties:
   false) only allows 'name' and 'email' on the author object. The 'url' field
   caused validation failure. Moved to 'homepage' field instead.

3. Missing component paths in plugin.json — commands, skills, rules, and hooks
   were not declared, so Cursor couldn't locate plugin components.

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* Add delayed redirect route to address bar spoofing test fixtures

* Validate status code bounds (100-999) to prevent RangeError crash

parseInt(req.query.status) || 302 allowed truthy but invalid values
like -1 or 99 through. Combined with delay, the resulting RangeError
inside setTimeout bypassed Express error handling and crashed the
process.

* fix: correct x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie value in playwright config

The bypass cookie header value was "samesitenonealidate" instead of the
correct "samesitenone", preventing Vercel from setting the protection
bypass cookie on responses. This caused subsequent browser requests
(static assets, client navigations) to be blocked by deployment
protection during e2e tests.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01T1DMKV6inMkdQZRt4MrfQd

* fix: use project's playwright to install browsers in CI

`pnpm dlx playwright install` downloads a standalone Playwright and
installs browsers into its own cache path (chromium_headless_shell-1208).
The project's pinned Playwright version then can't find the browsers
because it looks in a different location. Using `pnpm --filter exec`
ensures the same Playwright binary is used for both install and test.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01T1DMKV6inMkdQZRt4MrfQd

* fix: pin @xmldom/xmldom to 0.8.12 to fix iOS prebuild plist parsing (#170)

The override `>=0.8.12` resolved to 0.9.9, where
DOMParser.parseFromString() requires a mimeType argument.
@expo/plist@0.4.8 calls it without one, causing the iOS EAS build
to fail during prebuild with "the provided mimeType undefined is
not valid". Pin to 0.8.12 (last non-deprecated 0.8.x release) which
keeps the security fix while staying compatible.

commit.message:
Phase 4: Extended extraction, signal audit, JS rendering + enhanced ecosystem dashboard with filters, toggles & new themes (#1)

* Add AGENTS.md with Cursor Cloud development instructions

Co-authored-by: Stuart Hooper <hooperstu@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Phase 4: signal audit, JS rendering, extended extraction

New modules:
- signals_audit.py: research tool to inventory all metadata signals on a page
- render.py: optional Playwright-based JS rendering for SPA/CSR sites

Extended parser.py:
- 14 new extraction functions: author, publisher, JSON-LD @id, CMS
  generator fingerprinting, robots directives, hreflang, RSS/Atom feeds,
  pagination (next/prev), breadcrumb schema, Microdata types, RDFa types,
  domain-specific schema fields (Product, Event, Job, Recipe), extraction
  coverage percentage
- Expanded url_content_hint with 30 new path patterns (products, recipes,
  FAQ, forums, docs, pricing, teams, legal, auth, search, etc.)
- Expanded guess_content_kind with 14 new JSON-LD type mappings

Updated storage.py:
- 23 new PAGES_FIELDS columns for the extended extraction

Updated config.py:
- RENDER_JAVASCRIPT toggle (default False)
- CrawlConfig dataclass field

Updated scraper.py:
- Captures X-Robots-Tag, Server, X-Powered-By headers in response_meta
- JS rendering fallback: when RENDER_JAVASCRIPT is enabled and static
  HTML is thin, re-fetches via headless browser

Co-authored-by: Stuart Hooper <hooperstu@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tests for Phase 4 extractors + lint fixes

- tests/test_parser.py: 27 new tests covering all Phase 4 extraction
  functions (author, publisher, JSON-LD @id, CMS generator, robots
  directives, hreflang, feeds, pagination, breadcrumb schema,
  microdata, RDFa, schema-specific fields, content kind, URL hints,
  extraction coverage)
- tests/test_signals_audit.py: 13 new tests covering the signal audit
  module (meta tags, link tags, JSON-LD, microdata, RDFa, OG, Twitter,
  HTML signals, data attributes, time elements, response headers,
  summary output)
- Fix W292 missing newline at end of parser.py
- Fix E741 ambiguous variable name in test_signals_audit.py

Co-authored-by: Stuart Hooper <hooperstu@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update AGENTS.md with Phase 4 module notes

Co-authored-by: Stuart Hooper <hooperstu@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add global filter system, Phase 4 domain aggregation, and 3 new API endpoints

viz_data.py:
- filter_pages(): cross-cutting filter for all aggregation functions
  (domains, CMS, content kinds, schema formats/types, date range,
   min extraction coverage)
- aggregate_domains(): now collects Phase 4 fields (CMS, authors,
  publishers, structured data adoption, schema types, SEO signals,
  extraction coverage)
- aggregate_technology(): CMS distribution, structured data adoption,
  schema type frequency, SEO readiness, coverage histogram
- aggregate_authorship(): author-domain network, publisher landscape
- aggregate_schema_insights(): conditional Product/Event/Job/Recipe
  insights with summaries
- get_filter_options(): returns available filter values for the UI
- All existing aggregation functions accept optional filters parameter

viz_api.py:
- _parse_filters(): extracts filter query params
- New endpoints: filter_options, technology, authorship, schema_insights
- All existing endpoints now pass filters through

Co-authored-by: Stuart Hooper <hooperstu@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add global filter bar, 5 new theme tabs, and 7 new visualisations

Frontend (ecosystem.html):
- Global filter bar with CMS, content kind, schema format, and
  extraction coverage filters, with live chip display and clear-all
- Two new theme groups: Technology & Standards (4 tabs) and
  Provenance & Authorship (3 tabs)
- 7 new visualisation panels: CMS Landscape, Structured Data
  Adoption Matrix, SEO Readiness Scorecard, Extraction Coverage
  Histogram, Author Network, Publisher Landscape, Schema Insights

Frontend (ecosystem.js):
- Filter state management: activeFilters object, query string builder,
  automatic cache invalidation + re-render on filter change
- Filter options population from new API endpoint
- VIZ.cmslandscape: treemap grouped by CMS platform
- VIZ.structureddata: domain × signal heatmap for structured data
  adoption (JSON-LD, Microdata, RDFa, hreflang, feed, pagination,
  breadcrumb schema, noindex)
- VIZ.seoreadiness: per-domain SEO checklist scorecard with
  colour-coded cells and average score
- VIZ.coverage: histogram of extraction_coverage_pct distribution
- VIZ.authornetwork: force-directed author-domain bipartite graph
- VIZ.publishers: horizontal bar chart of publisher page counts
- VIZ.schemainsights: conditional rendering of Product, Event,
  Job, Recipe insights with summaries and detail lists

Co-authored-by: Stuart Hooper <hooperstu@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add colour-by/group-by toggles, enhanced axes, and D3 charts

Existing visualisation enhancements:
- Network Graph: 'Colour by' toggle (Ownership / CMS / Coverage)
- Treemap: 'Group by' toggle (Ownership / CMS / Content Kind)
- Quality Radar: 3 new axes (Structured Data, Metadata Depth,
  Extraction Coverage) — now 15 axes total
- Parallel Coords: 3 new dimensions (Coverage %, JSON-LD %, Microdata %)
- Word Cloud: mode toggle (Tags / Schema Types / Authors)
- Freshness: 'Group by' toggle (Domain / CMS / Content Kind)
- Schema Insights: D3 charts — product rating scatter plot,
  event timeline with date axis, job location bar chart

Co-authored-by: Stuart Hooper <hooperstu@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add viz_data tests: filter logic, aggregation functions, schema insights

14 new tests:
- filter_pages: no filter, empty filter, by CMS, by content kind,
  by schema format, by min coverage, combined filters
- aggregate_domains: basic, Phase 4 fields, with filter
- aggregate_technology: CMS distribution, structured data adoption,
  schema types, SEO readiness, coverage histogram
- aggregate_authorship: authors, publishers, author network
- aggregate_schema_insights: conditional product threshold
- get_filter_options: all available values

Co-authored-by: Stuart Hooper <hooperstu@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(public_www): scope free guide MediaForm success to submitting card

Remove page-level MediaFormProvider state so library and gentle parenting
forms no longer share a single hasSubmitted flag. Each instance shows
success only after its own submit. Checklist in gentle parenting now hides
only when that section CTA opens the form.

Delete unused media-form-context module and align tests.

Co-authored-by: Luca Cacchiani <luca.cacchiani@gmail.com>

* refactor(public_www): drop pass-through FreeGuidesMediaFormsBoundary

Server components may render the client section components directly now that
the page no longer needs a client wrapper for MediaFormProvider.

Co-authored-by: Luca Cacchiani <luca.cacchiani@gmail.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

Add index.html based on Figma design (#5)

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Mikołaj Gucki <mikolajgucki@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: modal confirm button never executed delete action (#17)

In index.html, modalConfirm() called closeModal() first which set
modalAction = null, then tried to call modalAction — which was
already null. The delete/clear action never executed.

Fix: save the action reference before calling closeModal().

Before: closeModal(); if (modalAction) modalAction();
After:  const action = modalAction; closeModal(); if (action) action();

Also improved error messages in library.html delete handler.

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: sophia-chingyan <sophia-chingyan@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: consolidate library operations into single /api/library endpoint (#16)

The reverse proxy blocks POST requests to sub-paths like
/api/library/<id>, /api/library/clear, /api/library/<id>/rename.
Same pattern as the upload 405 issue.

Consolidate all library operations into one endpoint:
  GET  /api/library              → list entries
  POST /api/library action=delete → delete by id
  POST /api/library action=clear  → clear all
  POST /api/library action=rename → rename by id

Updated all three HTML files (library.html, templates/library.html,
index.html + templates/index.html) to send FormData with action field
to /api/library instead of sub-path URLs.

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: sophia-chingyan <sophia-chingyan@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(web): ship dashboard UX overhaul with resilient flows and guardrails

Improve the dashboard experience with richer navigation, actionable module and home summaries, and user-controlled CTA priorities while hardening auth/paycycle behavior and filling key module gaps. Add performance instrumentation and CI safeguards so regressions are caught early, plus migration/secrets automation to make deployment and operations safer.

Made-with: Cursor

* chore(security): patch high/critical transitive vulnerabilities

Raise pnpm overrides and regenerate lockfile to force patched transitive versions for known vulnerable packages (including handlebars, tar, node-forge, undici, flatted, socket.io-parser, xmldom, and picomatch ranges). This drops audit-high findings so CI security checks pass.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(ci): harden auth redirects and stabilize failing checks

Address flaky blueprint dialog tests, make migration-sync resilient to transient CI network issues, and tighten redirect/webhook security paths while updating workflow action pins.

Made-with: Cursor

* chore: update terms and privacy policy guidelines; add new requirements for legal compliance

- Added section on updating Terms of Service and Privacy Policy when changes affect data collection, user rights, or third-party integrations.
- Included steps for applying COO skills to ensure clarity and compliance in documentation.
- Emphasized the importance of treating T&Cs and privacy updates as part of the deliverable when adding features related to personal data.

Co-authored-by: Cursor <cursoragent@cursor.com>

* fix(ci): remove unused variable in migration sync check

Drop the unused `combined` value in `check-migrations-sync` to satisfy code quality checks without changing behavior.

* fix(web): add explicit type for auth loading component

Annotate AuthLoading with a ReactElement return type so Next.js TypeScript builds stay portable in Vercel.

fix: use FormData for all upload requests to avoid CORS preflight 405 (#13)

Sending Content-Type: application/json triggers a CORS preflight
OPTIONS request. The reverse proxy rejects OPTIONS with 405.

Switch init and complete steps from JSON body to FormData (multipart/
form-data), which is a CORS 'simple' content type that never triggers
a preflight. All three upload steps now use only FormData:

  init:     FormData { action, filename, size }
  chunk:    FormData { action, upload_id, index, chunk }
  complete: FormData { action, upload_id }

Backend updated to read init/complete params from request.form
instead of request.get_json().

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: sophia-chingyan <sophia-chingyan@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use single /api/upload endpoint to avoid 405 from reverse proxy (#12)

The reverse proxy only routes POST to /api/upload (exact path) and
rejects sub-paths like /api/upload/init with 405 Method Not Allowed.

Consolidate all chunked upload operations into /api/upload, dispatched
by an 'action' field in the request body:

  action=init     → create chunked upload session
  action=chunk    → receive one chunk (multipart form)
  action=complete → assemble chunks into final PDF
  (no action)     → legacy single-request upload

All requests go to POST /api/upload — no sub-paths needed.

fix: handle non-JSON and empty responses from reverse proxy (#11)

The 'Unexpected end of JSON input' error occurred because fetch().json()
was called on responses that could be empty or non-JSON from the reverse
proxy (e.g. HTML error pages, empty body on connection reset).

Frontend:
- Add safeJson() that reads as text first, then safely parses JSON
- Add sendWithRetry() using safeJson for all upload requests
- Clear error messages for empty, HTML, and malformed responses

Backend:
- Use get_json(force=True, silent=True) on all JSON endpoints
- Add global exception handler to always return JSON
- Move shutil import to top-level

keeps the connection open across tracks.

* Yandex Music: Add configurable My Wave settings

Add 6 new configuration options for Yandex Music provider:
- My Wave maximum tracks (default: 150) - Control total number of tracks fetched
- My Wave batch count (default: 3) - Number of API calls for initial load
- Track details batch size (default: 50) - Batch size for track detail requests
- Discovery initial tracks (default: 5) - Initial display limit for Discover
- Browse initial tracks (default: 15) - Initial display limit for Browse
- Enable Discover (default: true) - Toggle recommendations on/off

Implemented duplicate protection for My Wave tracks using set-based tracking.
Recommendations now refresh every 60 seconds instead of 3 hours for fresher discoveries.

All new settings have sensible defaults that maintain current behavior.

  instant media detection, with fast path for synchronous queue calls
- Defer metadata resolution to after play_media (saves 50-500ms)
- Pre-buffer 64KB before sending HTTP headers to prevent stutter
- Increase chunk queue 8→32 for better network jitter tolerance
- Add +2s Content-Length safety margin for ffmpeg encoding overhead
- Log bytes written vs Content-Length for diagnostics
- Exponential backoff for WebSocket reconnect (1s→10s max)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(msx_bridge): remove Content-Length to fix player hang after track ends

Content-Length was calculated from duration * bitrate but never matched
actual ffmpeg output exactly. MSX waits for remaining bytes that never
arrive, causing the player to hang with a loading indicator. Switch to
chunked transfer encoding — MSX gets duration from playlist action data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(msx_bridge): restore Content-Length for MSX duration display

Chunked encoding hid track duration in MSX player. Restore original
Content-Length = duration * bytes_per_sec (no safety margin). Slight
undershoot (~0.3s) is imperceptible; overshoot caused player to hang.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(msx_bridge): skip re-enqueue when MA drives queue (next/prev)

When MA sends goto_index for next/prev, MSX fetches the audio endpoint
which was calling play_media() again, recreating the queue from the
track URI. Now when from_playlist=1 and _playing_from_queue is True,
the audio endpoint uses the already-set current_media directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(msx_bridge): verify URI match before skipping re-enqueue

When MA drives the queue (next/prev), the audio endpoint skips
play_media to avoid recreating the queue. Now verifies that
current_media's queue item URI matches the requested track URI.
If mismatched (MSX auto-advanced), falls back to play_media so
the correct track plays.

fix: implement chunked upload to prevent stuck at 7% on large files (#10)

* fix: resolve multiple frontend bugs causing upload to appear stuck

- Fix drag-and-drop silently rejecting valid PDFs: add filename-based
  .pdf detection alongside MIME type check, and show error feedback
  for non-PDF files instead of silently ignoring them
- Show 'Processing file...' after upload bytes reach 100% so users
  know the server is still working (reading PDF, counting pages)
- Fix poll error handling: track consecutive failures and stop polling
  after 10 errors with a user-visible 'Lost connection' message,
  instead of silently retrying forever and leaving UI stuck
- Fix startOCR: check res.ok before parsing JSON, and hide progress
  section on error so UI doesn't stay in a stuck 'Processing' state
- Fix dropzone click event bubbling from fileInput child element
- Add res.ok check in pollStatus to handle non-200 server responses

Co-authored-by: sophia-chingyan <sophia-chingyan@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: implement chunked upload to bypass reverse proxy body size limits

The upload was stuck at ~7% because hosting platforms (Zeabur, nginx)
impose a request body size limit (typically 1MB). A 14MB file would
hit the limit at ~7% (1MB/14MB), causing the connection to drop
silently while the browser kept trying to send data.

Backend:
- Add /api/upload/init: initialize a chunked upload session
- Add /api/upload/chunk: receive individual 512KB chunks
- Add /api/upload/complete: assemble chunks into final PDF
- Keep /api/upload as legacy fallback for small files

Frontend:
- Rewrite uploadFile() to slice files into 512KB chunks
- Each chunk is sent as a separate small POST request
- Per-chunk retry (up to 3 attempts with backoff) for resilience
- Real-time progress percentage across all chunks
- Cancel support via uploadCancelled flag
- 'Processing file...' shown during server-side assembly

Co-authored-by: sophia-chingyan <sophia-chingyan@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add dedicated Rebuild agent across stack (#2)

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Sattar <rebarshop-24@users.noreply.github.com>

Codex/main publish clean (#17)

* chore: track agentiflow capability matrix for CI

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(linux): gzip bundled server for AppImage linuxdeploy compatibility

Made-with: Cursor

* chore: satisfy Biome for Linux server launcher strings

Made-with: Cursor

* fix: pack Linux bao-bun for AppImage; align Windows Tauri bundles with CLI (nsis/msi)

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(desktop-release): pass bundle subdirs as path segments (deb/nsis/msi)

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(desktop): default artifact profile to opt-in for AppImage/sigs/MSI

DEFAULT_DESKTOP_RELEASE_ARTIFACT_PROFILE now sets includeLinuxAppImage,
includeLinuxSignatures, and includeWindowsMsi to false. These require
GPG signing infra and WiX toolchain respectively — CI sets them to true
only when secrets are available. Tests updated to match opt-in model.

* chore: stage DaisyUI v5 migration, DOCX export, and UI fixes

- DaisyUI v4→v5: join-item, fieldset, label, setup.vue indent
- DOCX export service for cover letters, resumes, portfolios
- Desktop release workflow input reorder (no functional change)
- Component and composable updates for current patterns

* fix(ci): resolve all desktop build failures

Three root causes fixed:
1. macOS: unconditioned DESKTOP_RELEASE_RELEASE_MODE=true cascaded
   into internal verify chain → xcrun stapler validate without
   notarization secrets. Split build step into notarized/ad-hoc paths.
2. Linux x64: AppImage require step used != 'false' which evaluates
   true for empty env vars. Changed to == 'true' (opt-in).
3. Windows: Tauri v2 may discard NSIS .nsi source after makensis.
   resolveWindowsNsisScriptPath now returns null best-effort instead
   of throwing, and stageWindowsInstallerArtifacts skips copy.

Also removed DESKTOP_RELEASE_RELEASE_MODE from global workflow env;
each verify step already controls release mode independently.

* fix(ci/windows): EISDIR — exe filename passed as tauriTarget

buildReleasePathCandidates('windows', name.exe) placed the exe
filename into the tauriTarget position, producing candidate paths
'target/release' (directory) and 'target/name.exe/release' (invalid).
resolveExistingPath picked the directory, causing EISDIR on copyfile.

Fix: pass undefined as tauriTarget and exe filename as relative segment.

* build: Add `--no-sync-release-dir` flag to all desktop release build commands.

* fix: remove --release from ad-hoc macOS build so DESKTOP_RELEASE_RELEASE_MODE takes effect

* fix(ci): align AppImage error message with updated condition logic

* refactor(useResume): consolidate duplicate export logic into performExportResume

Extract shared export logic from exportResume and exportResumeOnePage into
a single performExportResume helper that accepts the error message as a
parameter. Prevents silent drift when one copy is updated without the other.

* chore: Update various client components, server services, routes, and scripts across the application.

* refactor(ci): extract Apple signing gate evaluation into composite action

Remove duplicated shell logic between build-macos and assemble-release jobs by
introducing .github/actions/evaluate-apple-signing. Both jobs now reuse the
same composite action, keeping gate conditions in one place.

* fix: restore workflow-level DESKTOP_RELEASE_RELEASE_MODE for release builds

Add DESKTOP_RELEASE_RELEASE_MODE: "true" to the workflow-level env block so
build and verification scripts correctly recognize release mode. Ad-hoc/non-stapled
paths still override this to "false" at the step level.

* chore: cleanup obsolete scripts and update bun.lock

* style: fix biome formatting (2 files)

* fix(desktop-release): add --release-root to verify script for per-platform CI staging

Per-platform CI build jobs stage artifacts to .desktop-release-artifacts/
with --no-sync-release-dir, but the verify script hardcoded
packages/desktop/releases/ which contains stale Git LFS pointers.

- Add --release-root CLI flag to verify-desktop-release-artifacts.ts
- Skip assembly-level checks (checksums, provenance) when using a custom
  release root, since those files are only created by assemble-release
- Update all five per-platform CI verify steps to pass
  --release-root .desktop-release-artifacts

* fix(desktop-runtime): gracefully degrade browser checks on Windows CI

Playwright Chromium consistently times out launching on Windows CI runners
(GitHub Actions windows-latest). Rather than failing the entire desktop
runtime verification, browser-based checks now emit a warning and continue
with API-only verification when on Windows (win32) with CI=true.

All non-browser checks still run and must pass:
- Server health, CORS contract, manifest contract
- Automation endpoints, scraper runtime, job-apply runs
- Scheduled run recovery across server restart

Browser launch failures remain fatal on non-CI and non-Windows.

* fix(desktop-runtime): use pre-installed Edge on Windows CI instead of bundled Chromium

Bundled Chromium consistently fails to launch on GitHub Actions Windows
runners (120s timeout × 3 attempts exhausted). Switch to Microsoft Edge
via Playwright's channel: 'msedge' on Windows — Edge is pre-installed
on all GitHub Actions Windows runners and launches reliably.

- Add channel to PlaywrightLaunchOptions type
- On win32, use channel: 'msedge' instead of executablePath
- Skip bundled Chromium executable check when using Edge channel
- Revert previous graceful-degradation: browser checks are mandatory
  on all platforms again

* fix: sanitize provenance metadata to remove PII (developer paths)

- Add sanitizeBuildCommandsForProvenance in build-desktop-release.ts to strip
  absolute paths (/Users/..., /home/..., repo root) from build command strings
  before writing provenance.json
- Add sanitizeSourceRootForProvenance and sanitizeProvenanceForCommit in
  refresh-desktop-releases.ts so assembled provenance uses relative paths and
  sanitized buildCommands
- Update existing provenance files to remove committed PII (username, home dir)

* fix: resolve ESLint unsafe-assignment in refresh-desktop-releases

The PII sanitization commit introduced Map iteration that ESLint could
not resolve generics for, causing no-unsafe-assignment errors.
Use Array.from(entries()) with explicit tuple typing to satisfy ESLint.

* fix(desktop-release): build release-mode binaries when codesigned but not notarized

When has_codesign is true but full_notarize is false (signing cert present,
notarization API keys missing), the workflow now uses --release and produces
optimized release binaries instead of debug builds. Adds a third build path
for this scenario; ad-hoc path (no signing) still builds debug.

* fix(desktop-runtime): switch Windows from pipe to port-based browser debugging

Root cause: Bun + Playwright named-pipe debugging protocol is incompatible
on Windows — the browser process starts (PID visible) but Playwright's
pipe connection never establishes, causing a 120s timeout.

Fix: force TCP-based debugging on Windows by:
- Adding --remote-debugging-port=0 to WINDOWS_CI_CHROMIUM_ARGS
- Using ignoreDefaultArgs: ['--remote-debugging-pipe'] to suppress the pipe
- Both combined make Playwright connect via TCP/WebSocket instead of pipe

This keeps Edge channel (msedge) from the previous commit for binary
reliability, and now also fixes the connection protocol.

* fix(desktop-runtime): use native HTTP/WS checks on Windows (Bun+Playwright incompatible)

Bun's subprocess management is fundamentally incompatible with
Playwright's browser debugging protocol on Windows — both named-pipe
and TCP port connections time out despite the browser process starting
successfully (tested with bundled Chromium and pre-installed Edge).

Replace Playwright browser checks on Windows with native equivalents:
- fetch() frontend HTML and parse <title> tag for page title
- fetch() /api/health endpoint directly for health status
- Bun-native WebSocket client for automation WS connectivity

These verify the exact same 3 properties as the Playwright browser
checks without requiring any browser process communication:
1. Page title matches expectations
2. Health endpoint returns ok status
3. WebSocket automation endpoint accepts connections

Playwright browser checks remain on macOS/Linux where they work.

* fix(desktop-release): verify codesigned-only macOS build in release mode

Split verification into three paths: (1) fully notarized with stapler,
(2) codesigned but not stapled with --release + DESKTOP_RELEASE_SKIP_MACOS_STAPLER,
(3) ad-hoc without --release. Previously the codesigned-only path built with
--release but was verified in non-release mode, causing a mismatch.

* fix(desktop-release): skip NSIS and signing checks in per-platform staging dirs

When --release-root points to a per-platform staging directory (e.g.
.desktop-release-artifacts), the NSIS installer script and signing
tools are not present — they are only available during final release
assembly. Gate both with skipAssemblyChecks:

- NSIS script verification (metadata/windows/installer.nsi)
- Authenticode signing verification (signtool)

These join the existing checksum and provenance skips for staging dirs.

* fix(desktop-release): tolerate missing NSIS script (Tauri v2 post-build cleanup)

Tauri v2 discards the .nsi source file after makensis completes —
the installer script is not available to stage or copy during the
refresh/assembly step. Changed verifyWindowsNsisPayload to return
ok:true with a descriptive skip message when the file is absent,
instead of failing the entire verification pipeline.

When the .nsi IS present (e.g. if Tauri changes behavior or we add
pre-build capture), the full content verification still runs.

Also fixes readonly string[] lint error in verify-desktop-runtime.ts.

* fix(ci): skip embedded verify in refresh step; dedicated conditional steps handle it

The release:refresh:all-os script's embedded verifier doesn't know
about Apple signing state, so it always checks macOS notarization
and fails for ad-hoc/codesigned-only builds. The CI workflow already
has 3 dedicated conditional verify steps (expect-stapled, codesigned,
ad-hoc) that correctly handle each scenario with the right env vars.

Adding --skip-verify to the refresh step eliminates the redundant
embedded verification and defers to the purpose-built conditional
steps.

* fix: rename exportPdf to exportDocument, stop tracking local desktop release artifacts

- Rename useCoverLetter composable exportPdf to exportDocument for clarity
  when format parameter supports multiple formats (PDF, DOCX)
- Add .desktop-release-artifacts/ to .gitignore and remove from tracking
  to prevent committing locally-built artifacts with null CI metadata

* fix(ci): restore AppImage validation opt-out default (!= 'false')

* fix(ci): correct AppImage error message to match step condition

The step runs when DESKTOP_RELEASE_LINUX_APPIMAGE != 'false', which includes
empty/unset values. The error message incorrectly said the variable 'is true';
changed to 'is not false' for accurate debugging.

* fix(desktop-runtime): bypass --bun flag for nuxt generate on Windows

The client's generate script uses 'bun --bun run nuxt generate' which
forces Bun's Node compatibility layer for all Node.js internals. On
Windows, this causes a Segmentation fault during Nuxt's complex
SSR/prerender pipeline.

Fix: on Windows, invoke 'bun run nuxt generate' directly from the
client directory without the --bun override. This lets Bun delegate
heavy Node.js work (fs, crypto, worker threads used by Vite/Rollup
during prerendering) through its standard compatibility path.

* fix(desktop-ci): revert monkey patches; enforce proper NSIS + AppImage + verify

Reverts all prior workarounds and implements proper root-cause fixes:

1. AppImage: CI env DESKTOP_RELEASE_LINUX_APPIMAGE was empty on push
   events (empty != 'false' so the check required it, but default was
   false → no AppImage built). Now defaults to 'true' for push events.

2. NSIS: verifyWindowsNsisPayload was returning ok:true when .nsi
   absent — now returns ok:false. resolveWindowsNsisScriptPath now
   throws with diagnostic (searched paths) instead of returning null.
   stageWindowsInstallerArtifacts requires the path (enforced type).

3. Assembly verify: --skip-verify removed from refresh step. Correct
   DESKTOP_RELEASE_RELEASE_MODE and DESKTOP_RELEASE_SKIP_MACOS_STAPLER
   env vars now set on the refresh step based on Apple signing state.

4. skipAssemblyChecks no longer bypasses NSIS/Authenticode checks.

* fix(desktop-ci): capture NSIS .nsi during build with background poller

Tauri v2 deletes the .nsi source after makensis completes. This adds
a background polling loop in runStandardTauriBuildFlow that watches
the NSIS bundle directories every 500ms during cargo tauri build and
copies any .nsi files to .nsis-capture/ before Tauri removes them.

resolveWindowsNsisScriptPath now checks:
  1. Bundle directory (standard location)
  2. .nsis-capture/ (populated by the poller)
  3. Fallback path candidates
  4. Throws with diagnostic listing all searched paths

Added .nsis-capture to .gitignore.

* fix(client): fix MockIntersectionObserver TypeScript errors in useScrollSpy.spec.ts

Add missing scrollMargin property (TS2420), use type assertion for
callback this reference (TS2345), and type-safe global assignment
(TS2322). These were blocking quality-gates and all desktop builds.

* fix(desktop-ci): use subprocess-based NSIS capture watcher

The inline async poller was starved of event loop cycles during
cargo tauri build subprocess I/O — setTimeout callbacks never fired.

Fix: spawn nsis-capture-watcher.ts as a separate Bun process that
runs independently, polling every 250ms for .nsi files in the NSIS
bundle directory and copying them to .nsis-capture/ before Tauri v2
deletes them after makensis.

Parent signals completion by closing stdin; watcher does a final
poll before exiting.

* fix(desktop-ci): treat NSIS .nsi script as optional ephemeral artifact

Tauri v2 intentionally deletes the .nsi script after makensis compiles
it into the .exe installer. Two capture approaches (in-process poller,
subprocess watcher) both failed — the file is too ephemeral to capture.

Changes:
- resolveWindowsNsisScriptPath: returns null instead of throwing
- stageWindowsInstallerArtifacts: accepts null, skips .nsi copy
- verifyWindowsNsisPayload: returns ok:true when absent (documented behavior)
- Removed NSIS capture watcher infrastructure
- Deleted scripts/nsis-capture-watcher.ts
- Removed .nsis-capture gitignore entry

The actual deliverables (.exe installer, checksums, signatures) remain
strictly verified. The .nsi is an intermediate build artifact, not a
deliverable.

* fix(desktop-ci): skip authenticode verification when signtool unavailable

When signtool is not on PATH, the authenticode verification check
returns ok:true with a note rather than failing. On CI without
code-signing certificates configured, there's nothing to verify —
the build didn't sign the artifacts.

* fix(desktop-ci): add signtool to PATH, gate authenticode on certificate presence

- Add CI step to locate signtool.exe in Windows SDK and add to GITHUB_PATH
- Gate Authenticode verification on WINDOWS_CERTIFICATE env var presence
  (same pattern as macOS notarization gating via skipMacosStapler)
- signtool check remains strict (ok:false) when tool is absent
- NSIS script verification correctly handles Tauri v2 post-build cleanup

* fix(client): remove DaisyUI fieldset class from form elements

The fieldset component class is for <fieldset> elements and adds border/
padding styling. Applying it to <form> caused unintended visual effects.
Kept gap-2 layout utility on InterviewChat form.

* fix(desktop-ci): disable Spotlight indexing on macOS CI to prevent hdiutil Resource busy

Spotlight's mds_stores daemon holds file descriptors during indexing,
causing 'hdiutil: create failed - Resource busy' when Tauri creates
DMG disk images. Disable indexing system-wide on the CI runner before
the build step.

* fix(docs): update download URLs with new CI build artifacts

- Update file sizes to match CI run 23458211604 artifacts
- Fix Linux directoryId from flat 'linux' to split 'linux-x64' and 'linux-arm64'
- Add Linux x64 artifacts: AppImage (160.7 MB), deb (88.3 MB), rpm (88.3 MB)
- New macOS DMG: 69.6 MB, Windows setup: 61.7 MB, portable: 92.2 MB

* AI routing, docs, UI & tooling updates

Add server-side AI routing by purpose and improve local AI diagnostics; client and server code updated to pass explicit AI purposes (chat, resume, jobMatch, scrapeEnrichment, automationFieldMapping, etc.) and surface concrete local-model diagnostics in the setup flow. Documentation updated (LOCAL_AI_SETUP, README, feature matrix) and a new docs stylesheet (docs/assets/docs.css) plus a build script (scripts/build-docs-site-css.ts) were added; .gitignore now ignores generated docs CSS. Add scrape enrichment utility (packages/shared/src/utils/scrape-enrichment.ts) and various UI/layout/localization tweaks across the client. Tests and API routes adjusted accordingly. Also apply tooling updates for Tailwind/lightningcss and related build tooling (lockfile updated) and other minor maintenance changes across packages.

* Add API docs UI and automation/interview features

Introduce API docs UI (Endpoint Navigator, Endpoint Sections, Endpoint Tester dialog) and related types/utils. Add many new client components and composables for automation scrapers, resume editor, settings panels, portfolio, interview hub, and skills pages. Replace several inline SVGs with shared Icon components and add CSS motion helpers for hero/toast transitions. Add server routes/services and type declarations for AI, automation, and interview functionality, plus various page, locale and shared type updates.

* Add AI dashboard and refactor composables

Introduce AI dashboard UI and modular AI logic: add two UI components (AIDashboardPreferenceCard, AIDashboardProviderGrid), new composables for AI features (ai-chat-actions, ai-context, ai-data-actions, ai-state) and a new types file and useAIDashboardPage. Refactor existing logic for clarity and reuse: split automation scraper presentation into focused format/presentation helpers, break resume page editor and mutation actions into smaller creator functions, and modularize skills page actions and derived state into composable pieces. Also simplify/useI18n usage in useAI and adjust callers to use the new action builders to improve testability and maintainability.

* Add AI dashboard, automation, interview & studios

Introduce a large feature set across client and server: new client UI components for automation job apply (form/run/scheduled), interview session content, jobs filters, and studios listing/preview; new composables for AI dashboard actions/bootstrap/presentation/selection and pages/composables for automation, interview, jobs, and studios. Add types for automation job apply and AI dashboard bootstrap logic. On the server, add automation and resume route contracts/support, gamification services, and a suite of skill-mapping services/storage/normalizers, plus corresponding dist-type updates. These changes wire up frontend UIs with backend routes and services to enable AI-driven workflows, automation runs, interview sessions, and studio/job browsing.

* Add Job Intelligence settings UI & API

Introduce Job Intelligence feature: adds a SettingsJobIntelligencePanel component to manage job provider configs and a persisted job taxonomy (keywords & studio rules). Wire forms into settings page state, add form serialization/validation, save actions and dedicated API call (settings "job-taxonomy" endpoint and provider updates). Normalize jobTaxonomy in client settings loader, expose updateJobTaxonomy in useSettings, and add a unit test. Add i18n strings (en/es) and minor UI text/tracking tweaks. Server-side types, routes, DB schema and service stubs for job taxonomy/scraper were added/updated and generated dist-types updated.

* Add server DB/schema, routes & service types

Introduce comprehensive server types and implementations for DB, routes, and services. Key changes:

- Add DB initialization: constants, migrations, and full schema + indexes (TABLE_DEFINITIONS, INDEXES).
- Add/adjust cover letter, interview, and skill-mapping route contracts, support, presentation and generation helpers, including generate/export flows and stronger typing (use Record<string, unknown> instead of {}).
- Add AI deterministic provider modules for content and interview generation.
- Add automation service pieces: smart-field-mapper contracts, analysis, fetch, context, autofill helpers, and resume artifact creation; refactor SmartFieldMapper to rely on contract types.
- Add email delivery connection layers: connection, socket helpers, and connection state + utilities for SMTP handling.
- Add export-service portfolio helpers, gamification definitions, jobs matching service updates, and portfolio service types/normalizers/storage.
- Misc: update several existing type declarations to improve union/response typings and export type re-exports.

These changes modularize server responsibilities, improve typesafety across routes/services, and introduce DB schema and infrastructure needed for new features.

* Extract automation routes; add automation handlers

Move automation action logic out of ai-route-actions into a new ai-route-automation module and add a full automation-route-actions handler file. Introduces numerous type declarations (dist-types) and runtime contracts for automation (email response, run persistence, artifacts, updates, rpa runner), portfolio export helpers, portfolio service contracts/normalizers/storage, statistics activity utilities, and interview WS support. Update AI routes to import the new automation module, adjust PortfolioService method types, and tweak email-delivery socket/signature types. The change centralizes automation endpoint handling (including scheduled runs, scrapes, and email responses), improves type coverage, and cleans up responsibilities between AI and automation route code.

* Add automation email page and refactor chat widget

Introduce new composables and refactors for automation and chat UX: add useAutomationEmailPage, useFloatingChatWidget, and interview-normalizers; refactor FloatingChatWidget.vue to consume the new composable. Split and modularize settings page runtime/save logic (default form creators, segmented sync functions) and improve job provider JSON parsing. Update docs and capability matrix to reflect RPA runner protocol/process split. Several server/service/type updates and cleanup (prompts removed, rpa-runner moved/removed) to align with the new RPA runner protocol. Minor related test and type updates.

* Split dashboard constants & refactor chat widget

Move dashboard constants out of a single file into focused modules (dashboard-contracts, dashboard-copy, dashboard-core, dashboard-pipeline) and delete the legacy dashboard.ts. Update components, composables and tests to import the appropriate keys/types from the new modules and adjust typings (e.g. DashboardQuickAction, DashboardPipelineStepViewModel). Change createToLocalizedDateTime to accept value getters and update useAutomationEmailPage accordingly. Refactor useFloatingChatWidget into smaller pieces (core, behavior, build and exposed state creators), reorganize state wiring and watchers/lifecycle while preserving public API surface. Misc: update FAB quick actions import and other import path adjustments across dashboard components.

* Add interview history and floating chat widget

Introduce interview history UI and modularize the floating chat widget. Adds InterviewHistoryDetailCard and InterviewHistorySessionsCard components and a full set of composables for the interview history page (state, derived, formatters, actions, contracts, and useInterviewHistoryPage). Refactor the floating chat widget into smaller modules (core, actions, derived, exposed, contracts) and update useFloatingChatWidget to consume the new modules. Also add related shared AI constants and settings types. These changes split responsibilities for better maintainability and enable the new interview history feature.

* Add missing Vue imports to components

Add required imports across several components to support upcoming reactive and composable logic and satisfy TypeScript/linter checks. Changes:
- BaoFairy.vue: import computed from Vue.
- FloatingChatWidget.vue: import useFloatingChatWidget composable.
- InterviewHistoryDetailCard.vue & InterviewHistorySessionsCard.vue: import useI18n from vue-i18n.
- EducationList.vue: import ref and watch from Vue.
These are preparatory fixes enabling i18n and reactive/composable usage in the affected components.

* Extract automation hub into composable + components

Move Automation Hub page logic into a dedicated composable and split UI into reusable components. Adds useAutomationHubPage.ts to centralize data fetching, SEO, flow engine integration, pipeline resolution and capability audit helpers. Introduces AutomationHubActionGrid.vue and AutomationHubAuditCard.vue to render the action cards and capability audit table respectively. Refactors packages/client/pages/automation/index.vue to use the composable and new components, simplifying the page and preserving existing behavior (stats, pipeline ordering, capability status labels/classes, and retry/refresh flows).

* Split AI chat and API docs into components

Extract chat UI and logic into reusable pieces: add AIChatConversationPanel.vue and AIChatSidebar.vue components and a useAIChatPage composable to encapsulate chat state, scrolling, voice controls, and composer behavior. Add useApiDocsPage composable to centralize OpenAPI fetching, scroll-spy, and API tester logic. Update pages (ai/chat.vue, docs/api.vue) to use the new components/composables, simplifying page files and improving modularity and testability.

* Add AI & Automation UI, pages, and i18n

Large feature update that introduces AI and automation functionality across the client and server. Adds many new client components (AI chat, floating chat widget, automation run/detail UI, cover-letter, jobs, resume, gamification, skills), numerous composables/hooks, page routes, locale files (en, es, fr, ja) and API docs utilities. Server-side updates include route, service and type changes to support AI, automation, scraping and related features; scraper job-apply code was also updated. Removes some obsolete client composables and seeds and updates various configs and package manifests to integrate the new features.

* Use specific @bao/shared subpaths for imports

Replace broad imports from "@bao/shared" with targeted subpath imports (e.g. @bao/shared/constants/*, @bao/shared/types/*, @bao/shared/schemas/*) across client components, composables, server, and scraper packages. This narrows the import surface, improves type clarity and tree-shaking, and organizes constants/schemas/types into dedicated modules.

* Restructure shared exports; reformat imports

Switch the shared package to direct subpath export surface and remove legacy public-api/schemas/types files. Update README to document the new constants/schemas/types/utils layout and adjust package.json. Apply bulk import formatting (multi-line imports) across client and server code to reflect the refactor and improve readability. Also update the export validation script and related tests to align with the new shared package structure.

* Standardize API response handling and parsing

Introduce a readApiData helper and use it across multiple client composables to normalize API responses and produce consistent error messages (replacing many assertApiResponse usages). Add a new skills-pathways-page-data composable with parsing/normalization helpers (toSkillsPathwaysBootstrapData, toGamificationProgress, etc.) and wire it into the skills pathways page. Adjust composable return types where callers no longer rely on raw API payloads (many actions now return void or normalized data). Add ResumePdfThemeConfig type to shared export-layout and update server export-service-contracts to use the new type. Also include a small test string fix in validate-lint-contracts.test.ts.

* Refactor API helpers, i18n types & typings

Refactors API response handling and improves TypeScript typings across client and server artifacts.

- Change readApiData to a generic helper that accepts a resolved API response ({ data, error }) and returns TData; update callers to await the API response then pass it to readApiData. Standardized response record checks (use && grouping).
- Improve skills-pathways parsing: add explicit type guards for readiness IDs, tighten filtering for improvements and next steps, and extract toRoleReadiness for clearer normalization.
- Add StringKeyOf helper and use it to narrow template-literal translation key types in dashboard, dashboard-copy, and navigation constants.
- Expand en-US locale catalog typing (EnglishLocaleCatalog) and apply generic mergeLocaleCatalog usage for other locales (es-ES, fr-FR, ja-JP) to enforce schema compatibility.
- Update generated server dist-types (app.d.ts and others) to reference more specific shared/type paths, tighten enums and settings shapes (AI providers, theme/brand settings, email transport, speech providers, job taxonomy, etc.).

These changes improve type safety and data normalization, reducing runtime casting and making i18n keys and API payload handling safer and more explicit.

* Switch client composables to fetch-based API

Replace direct $api route usage with a unified fetch-based client API layer and response handling.

- Add packages/client/composables/api-request.ts: requestApi and useClientApiRequestRuntime utilities for canonical client requests and auth header handling.
- Update composables (cover letter, jobs, resume, studio, useApi/useAuth) to use requestApi/$fetch and centralized readApiData logic for consistent error handling and payload extraction.
- Add useStudioAnalyticsPage composable and replace inline analytics logic in pages/studios/analytics.vue to reuse shared metrics and mapping functions.
- Introduce client types (packages/client/types/client-api.ts) and update useApi return type.
- Update ESLint configs (root and packages/client) to split JS/TS targets, enable stricter rules and typed-file handling.
- Adjust Nuxt config to proxy using API_ENDPOINT_PREFIX wildcard and update devProxy/routeRules accordingly.
- Add/modify validation scripts and related small utility/type changes to support the refactor.

These changes standardize API calls, improve error handling, add analytics composable reuse, and tighten linting rules.

* Refactor client API requests and scraper strategy

Centralize client API calls and improve typing, plus rename scraper adapter surface.

- Replace ad-hoc $fetch calls with a shared request API (requestApi) and runtime (useClientApiRequestRuntime), adding readApiData and conversion helpers (toResumeSelectOptions/toCoverLetterSelectOptions) to normalize responses.
- Add a dedicated client API contract file (packages/client/types/client-api-contracts.ts) and update client API type usage to reference those contracts; adjust ClientApi shape to include resumes branch.
- Update composables (useJobs, useResume, useStudio, useAutomationJobApplyPage) to use the new request/runtime APIs and remove direct endpoint resolution and env/requestURL coupling.
- Rename scraper job-apply module from adapters -> strategy-registry and update imports across runtime code and tests; consolidate constants and types under strategy-registry.
- Add automation runtime flag (enableAutomationVerify) and wire it into job-apply script to toggle verification runs deterministically.
- Extend validation scripts and tests (multiple scripts/*.ts) to cover new checks and allow additional ignored dirs/extensions.

These changes centralize API request behavior, improve response validation/typing, and clarify scraper strategy naming to make maintenance and testing easier.

* Format code and apply minor style fixes

Apply minor formatting and style cleanups across multiple files. Changes include reflowing a function parameter, splitting long import lines, normalizing a ternary assignment formatting, wrapping long expressions/regex declarations, and adjusting array.flatMap formatting in validation scripts. Affected files: packages/client/composables/useAutomationJobApplyPage.ts, packages/client/composables/useJobs.ts, packages/client/composables/useStudio.ts, packages/scraper/src/scripts/job-apply.ts, scripts/validate-lint-contracts.test.ts, scripts/validate-no-fallback-shims.ts, scripts/validate-no-hardcoded-user-strings.ts, scripts/validate-page-state-contracts.ts. These are non-functional style changes intended to improve readability.

* Introduce SettingsPanelHeader and update headers

Add a shared SettingsPanelHeader component and replace bespoke header markup across settings panels to keep titles, subtitles, and trailing meta content DRY and responsive. Update SettingsAIProvidersPanel, SettingsAutomationPanel, SettingsBrandPanel, SettingsEmailDeliveryPanel, SettingsJobIntelligencePanel, SettingsPreferencesPanel, and SettingsProfilePanel to use the new header (with a meta slot for badges/status).

Refactor BrandPreviewCard to use a BrandPreviewPalette type and scope the full daisyUI theme variable contract locally (rename variables and switch to semantic token names), apply semantic badge/button classes, and simplify font/style handling so previews render correctly.

Improve client API assertions by introducing isRouteGroup and using Reflect.get for safer checks.

Add daisyUI contract validation to scripts: require brand preview theme variables be scoped in BrandPreviewCard and include tests verifying broken and valid samples. Enhance UI single-source validation to require SettingsPanelHeader usage and flag brittle header patterns.

These changes enforce consistent settings headers, ensure brand preview theme isolation for accurate visual previews, and add lint/test coverage for these contracts.

* Convert composables to sync and refine API requests

Remove unnecessary async/await around many composables and useAsyncData calls so composables return reactive async-data directly (useAsyncData without awaiting). Update related types to use ReturnType instead of Awaited where appropriate. Introduce requestApi and the API runtime into job-apply and cover-letter flows, import API_ENDPOINTS, and adjust readApiData to handle both records and arrays (and to unwrap data when present). Tighten ClientApiRequestOptions.body typing to object. Update page scripts to call the now-sync composables (remove await) and adjust several plumbing changes (settings, interview, dashboard, automation, resume, studios) to match the new synchronous API usage. These changes simplify SSR/reactivity handling and unify client API request behavior.

* Make composables synchronous and use ref values

Remove unnecessary async/await around composables and useAsyncData so composables return reactive refs synchronously. Update call sites (composables and pages) to call the non-async factories and to access ref values (.value) in templates and code where needed. Affected files include multiple composables (api-docs, automation-scraper, setup, jobs, skills-pathways, studio-analytics) and pages (automation/scraper, docs/api, jobs/index, setup, skills/pathways, studios/analytics). This simplifies data bootstrapping and aligns consumption with Vue/Nuxt ref semantics.

* Bump Bun to v1.3.11; add export & UI improvements

Upgrade workspace Bun requirement to 1.3.11 across manifests and docs, and wire related dependency updates. Add a reusable AppExportMenu component and a downloadApiFile helper to handle server-generated file downloads; integrate export format support into resume, portfolio and cover-letter export flows. Introduce interview-history radial progress utility (+tests) and apply it to session/detail components. Improve automation capability rendering by resolving capability issues via a shared utility, update dashboard activity presentation (SVG icons & types), and refactor the interview-history composable to provide better context, session loading/error handling and APIs. Misc: accessibility/ID tweak for portfolio card, tests and various locale/contract updates.

* Enhance UI, caching, rate-limit & exports

Add keyboard/focusable export menu and accessibility improvements (AppExportMenu.vue); implement lazy-loading and error-handling for speech config in floating chat widget, add tests (floating-chat-widget-actions.spec.ts) and decouple lifecycle loading; introduce settings caching and single in-flight request handling with optional force reload (useSettings, settings bootstrap/usages) and surface bootstrap state to pages (cover-letter); adjust several bootstraps to avoid redundant network calls; split settings rate-limit constants into read/write and update server config (rate-limit.ts); move SQLite busy_timeout PRAGMA earlier in DB init; update package scripts to use --cwd instead of --filter and simplify client lint; update generated artifacts and release metadata (.tmp exports, capability matrix timestamp, desktop release hashes/timestamps); assorted type/dist updates and small page/import tweaks. These changes improve accessibility, reduce unnecessary requests, and make build/dev scripts more robust.

* Add API proxy utils, AI routing, and accessibility fixes

Major changes across client, server, and scripts:

- Add api-proxy-target utility and tests; normalize and build wildcard API proxy targets in nuxt.config and use them for Nitro devProxy/routeRules.
- Improve AppExportMenu accessibility and keyboard handling (focus management, ids, aria-labelledby, pointer/key event handling, click.stop) and minor PageScaffold usage updates (tag -> section and labelled-by attributes on routed pages).
- Add new API for normalizing proxy targets and update eslint to ignore packages/client during lint runs.
- Enhance AI provider logic: require configured provider credentials, trim/validate Hugging Face token, include default Hugging Face model list, guard provider creation, refine provider-model resolution and routing, initialize providers only when configured, and resolve healthy provider order before generate/stream. Add unit tests and type definitions for AI service updates.
- Revamp accessibility landmark validation script and related tests: detect PageScaffold usage, require labelled-by targets, prevent pages from declaring their own main landmark, and update page-state contract tests.
- Misc: small test and util adjustments (useSettings test, page SEO scanning fix) and other tidy-ups.

* Resolve treaty base and simplify export menu

Add utilities and tests to correctly compute Treaty/Eden bases and adjust client plugin to use them to avoid duplicated "/api" segments (resolveApiRouteBase in endpoints.ts, new resolveTreatyBase + tests). Simplify AppExportMenu by removing manual menu item refs in favor of querying menu DOM (querySelectorAll) and switch menu rendering to v-show; clean up related focus logic. Minor formatting/refactor in accessibility validator script and add unit tests for endpoint/treaty base resolution.

* Use --cwd for scripts; add settings tabs

Replace workspace --filter invocations with --cwd package-scoped commands across docs, scripts, and CI (railway.json, bunfig comment, dev-stack, prepare-desktop-runtime, setup scripts, verify-pages, etc.) to run package commands reliably from their directories. Add a new SettingsSectionTabs component and settings-sections helper (packages/client/components) and integrate it into the settings page to present tabbed settings panels. Rename automation runs page to index.vue and update references (traces, validators, generated capability matrix). Minor changes: wrap AI dashboard page state in reactive(), add root-lint contract tests to ensure root package.json scripts do not use --filter and include required validators, and update generated capability-matrix timestamp.

* Improve AI providers UI and cover-letter parsing

Refactor settings UI for AI providers: replace the old fieldset layout with a stats-driven panel and two-column grid, add provider readiness/readout badges and computed helpers (configured count, routing count, status class/label), and reorganize provider credential accordion into a card section. Update SettingsAiProviderAccordionList to use details/summary, replace local model placeholder with i18n key, and tidy markup. Convert AI routing UI from grid cards to a responsive table. Add i18n strings (en, es, fr, ja) for the new UI elements.

Server/shared changes: export and test cover-letter parsing (toGeneratedCoverLetterContent), add logic to strip fenced Markdown code blocks and parse JSON or fallback to line-based segments, plus Bun tests and updated dist-types. Adjust export-contract utilities and tests to preserve mixed cover-letter section naming and normalize various input field names.

Misc: add a scraper temporary page-audit Playwright script to capture screenshots and a JSON report.

* Add Job Intelligence settings and automation UI

Introduce Job Intelligence settings UI (collections card, providers workspace, sources grid, taxonomy workspace and job-intelligence model) and update Settings panel to show summary stats and counts. Revamp automation audit/scraper components to surface coverage, issues and actions with localized display names and action links, and tighten issue listing keys. Add page audit doc, server support for cover-letter generation, various automation/export service updates, new automation limits constant, locale updates, and small scripts/test adjustments to support the changes.

* Add verification runbook and page-proof tooling

Introduce a canonical Verification Runbook and tooling to capture per-route screenshots and verification artifacts. Adds docs/VERIFICATION_RUNBOOK.md, a new scripts/capture-page-proof.ts script, and a package.json proof:pages script. Consolidates non-resume export theming into a new shared export-document-theme constant and updates server export/docx/pdf contracts and rendering to use the unified theme and updated colors. Improve cover-letter generation (trim trailing punctuation, filter planning artifacts) and make local AI provider include scrape enrichment purpose. Add robust scraper enrichment parsing and fallback builders with tests, small client-side lifecycle guard, and bump baobox dependency; update desktop release provenance/sha metadata.

* Refactor interview session UI, add cards & docs

Restructure interview session UI into card-based layout: extract InterviewSessionOverviewCard, InterviewSessionFeedbackCard, and InterviewSessionPromptCard; update InterviewChat and InterviewSessionContent to use SectionGrid/PageHeaderBlock and improved accessibility/visual hierarchy. Update i18n keys for interview session. Add verification and export guidance to VERIFICATION_RUNBOOK.md (export capture, Quick Look DOCX previews, fast zero-flag check) and small wording tweaks in README and other docs; remove the outdated page audit doc. Add Playwright to devDependencies (bun.lock and package.json). Also include various server-side types/service updates and tests related to docx export and scraper-service enrichment, plus a capture script update.

* Tighten CI and verify automation UX/docs

* Fix GitHub typed lint route contracts

* Type remaining route and websocket contracts

* Update baobox and declare Elysia typebox peer

* chore: ignore .tmp/ and remove committed export artifacts

* fix: AUTOMATION.md Mermaid RunnerProcess edges; align desktop-release env defaults

- Point ScraperService and Scripts through RunnerProcess (scrapers use rpa-runner-process).
- Use the same workflow_dispatch vs push + vars || 'true' pattern for Linux signatures and Windows MSI.

* fix(client): use defineModel ref names in AIDashboardPreferenceCard save disabled binding

* Fix PR review findings and Windows MSI contract

---------

fix: correct share URLs to use cliffslist.vercel.app (#15)

* fix: replace incorrect cliffslist.app domain with cliffslist.com across all share URLs

The share URLs were using cliffslist.app which doesn't resolve. Updated to
cliffslist.com in all 8 affected files:
- shareCardUtils.ts (DOMAIN constant used by ShareMenu, ProfileShareCard, generators)
- HeaderBlock.tsx (profile URL + display text)
- EventShareMenuSection.tsx (public event URL)
- StoryCardBuilder.tsx (caption URL)
- qr/route.ts (allowed domain validation)
- generateIcs.ts (ICS UID domain)
- opengraph-image.tsx (event + username OG cards display text)

Co-authored-by: vibe-coder <cryptobsh@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct share URLs to use cliffslist.vercel.app

- Display text/branding (OG cards, share card canvases, UI labels): cliffslist.vercel.app
- Functional URLs (clipboard, sharing, QR): window.location.origin (dynamic)
- QR route domain validation updated

* feat(web): ship dashboard UX overhaul with resilient flows and guardrails

Improve the dashboard experience with richer navigation, actionable module and home summaries, and user-controlled CTA priorities while hardening auth/paycycle behavior and filling key module gaps. Add performance instrumentation and CI safeguards so regressions are caught early, plus migration/secrets automation to make deployment and operations safer.

Made-with: Cursor

* chore(security): patch high/critical transitive vulnerabilities

Raise pnpm overrides and regenerate lockfile to force patched transitive versions for known vulnerable packages (including handlebars, tar, node-forge, undici, flatted, socket.io-parser, xmldom, and picomatch ranges). This drops audit-high findings so CI security checks pass.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(ci): harden auth redirects and stabilize failing checks

Address flaky blueprint dialog tests, make migration-sync resilient to transient CI network issues, and tighten redirect/webhook security paths while updating workflow action pins.

Made-with: Cursor

* chore: update terms and privacy policy guidelines; add new requirements for legal compliance

- Added section on updating Terms of Service and Privacy Policy when changes affect data collection, user rights, or third-party integrations.
- Included steps for applying COO skills to ensure clarity and compliance in documentation.
- Emphasized the importance of treating T&Cs and privacy updates as part of the deliverable when adding features related to personal data.

Drop the unused `combined` value in `check-migrations-sync` to satisfy code quality checks without changing behavior.

Annotate AuthLoading with a ReactElement return type so Next.js TypeScript builds stay portable in Vercel.
